model_narrative: We allocate a set of GNMA mortgage pools to fill forward TBA delivery contracts. Each contract is filled through a sequence of delivery lots of fixed target size, and for each pool we decide how much of its face value to place into each contract's lot and how much to leave unallocated in the box. We also decide which lots to fill versus fail, which pools are placed in which lots, and which pools end up split across destinations. The objective is to maximize total profit, which rewards allocated face value by the spread between each contract's forward selling price and the current market price while charging a tiny penalty for splitting pools.

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the available GNMA mortgage pools, each identified by a pool number

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the TBA forward contracts that must be satisfied with delivered pools

sets.name: l
sets.doc: the delivery lots, given in fill order; for each contract the early lots are filled to a fixed size before the next lot receives anything

sets.name: c3
sets.doc: the subset of pools flagged as class-3 high-risk; these pools are restricted in how they may be used to fill a lot

params.name: head
params.doc: the first incremental allocation step size, in face-value units

params.name: steps
params.doc: the incremental step size used when building up an allocation, in face-value units

params.name: pv
params.doc: pool face values keyed by pool and attribute; the attribute 'original' gives the original face value and 'adjusted' gives the adjusted face value, both in face-value units

params.name: tbainfo
params.doc: contract attributes keyed by contract and attribute name: 'quantity' is the total face value the contract must be filled with, 'selling' and 'market' are the forward selling price and current market price quoted per 100 of face value, 'variation' is the fractional tolerance allowed around a lot's target size, and 'maxpools' is the baseline cap on how many distinct pools may be placed in a full-size lot

params.name: a
params.doc: the adjusted face value of each pool, in face-value units; this is the total amount of the pool that is available to be allocated or boxed

params.name: o
params.doc: the original face value of each pool, in face-value units

vars.name: profit
vars.doc: the total profit, in price-spread-weighted face-value units

vars.name: v
vars.doc: the face-value amount of a pool allocated to a given contract's lot

vars.name: b
vars.doc: the adjusted face value of a pool left unallocated in the box

vars.name: z
vars.doc: 1 if any of a pool is allocated to a given contract's lot, 0 otherwise

vars.name: w
vars.doc: 1 if a contract's lot is filled (passes), 0 if the lot is failed and left empty

vars.name: s
vars.doc: 1 if a pool is split across more than one destination, 0 otherwise

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each contract lot, when the lot is filled the total face value of all pools placed into that lot must reach at least its target size, allowing the lot to come in slightly under target by the contract's tolerance fraction. If the lot is failed it carries no such lower requirement.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each contract lot, when the lot is filled the total face value of all pools placed into that lot must not exceed its target size, allowing the lot to come in slightly over target by the contract's tolerance fraction. If the lot is failed nothing may be placed in it.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each pool, the face value it contributes across all contract lots plus the amount of it left in the box must together equal its total available adjusted face value.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each contract lot, when the lot is filled at least some face value coming from pools that are not flagged high-risk must be placed into it, so a filled lot cannot be made up entirely of high-risk pools.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each pool and contract lot, the face value of that pool placed into that lot can be positive only when the pool is marked as allocated to that lot, and even then it cannot exceed the largest amount that lot could hold at the top of its tolerance band.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each contract lot, the number of distinct pools allocated to it must not exceed the allowed cap on pools per lot, where the cap depends on the lot's target size.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: Total profit is the face value allocated to each contract's lots valued at that contract's spread between its forward selling price and the current market price, summed over every pool and lot, reduced by a tiny penalty charged for each pool that is split.

query: Risk wants us to lighten the book this cycle — hold the total face value we place across all four lots to no more than 3,000. Run that ceiling and tell me where the profit on the trade lands.
expected_pyomo: model.cap_total_value = Constraint(expr=sum(model.v[p, 20580252, l] for p in model.p for l in model.l) <= 3000)

query: Suppose settlement issues mean we can't fill either of the last two lots, l3 and l4. Run that scenario and tell me where profit lands.

query: Compliance is floating a rule to keep the class-3 high-risk pools out of every lot entirely — box them, don't allocate them. What's the profit hit if none of the class-3 pools can go into any lot?
expected_pyomo: model.no_c3 = Constraint(model.l, rule=lambda m, l: sum(m.z[p, 20580252, l] for p in m.c3) <= 0)

query: Every lot ends up stuffed with two or three pools, and reconciliation is a headache. Wouldn't it settle far cleaner if each lot carried just a single pool? What's keeping the model from putting one pool in each lot?
expected_pyomo: model.want_one_pool_each_lot = Constraint(model.l, rule=lambda m, l: sum(m.z[p, 20580252, l] for p in m.p) <= 1)

expected_pyomo: model.want_no_c3 = Constraint(model.l, rule=lambda m, l: sum(m.z[p, 20580252, l] for p in m.c3) <= 0)

vars.doc: the adjusted face value of a pool left unallocated in the box

vars.name: z
vars.doc: 1 if any of a pool is allocated to a given contract's lot, 0 otherwise

vars.name: w
vars.doc: 1 if a contract's lot is filled (passes), 0 if the lot is failed and left empty

vars.name: s
vars.doc: 1 if a pool is split across more than one destination, 0 otherwise

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each contract lot, when the lot is filled the total face value of all pools placed into that lot must reach at least its target size, allowing the lot to come in slightly under target by the contract's tolerance fraction. If the lot is failed it carries no such lower requirement.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each contract lot, when the lot is filled the total face value of all pools placed into that lot must not exceed its target size, allowing the lot to come in slightly over target by the contract's tolerance fraction. If the lot is failed nothing may be placed in it.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each pool, the face value it contributes across all contract lots plus the amount of it left in the box must together equal its total available adjusted face value.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each contract lot, when the lot is filled at least some face value coming from pools that are not flagged high-risk must be placed into it, so a filled lot cannot be made up entirely of high-risk pools.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each pool and contract lot, the face value of that pool placed into that lot can be positive only when the pool is marked as allocated to that lot, and even then it cannot exceed the largest amount that lot could hold at the top of its tolerance band.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: For each contract lot, the number of distinct pools allocated to it must not exceed the allowed cap on pools per lot, where the cap depends on the lot's target size.

constraints.name: l)
constraints.intent: Total profit is the face value allocated to each contract's lots valued at that contract's spread between its forward selling price and the current market price, summed over every pool and lot, reduced by a tiny penalty charged for each pool that is split.

query: Lot l1 ends up holding a handful of pools. Wouldn't a single clean pool per lot be simpler to settle? What's keeping the model from just dropping one pool into l1?
expected_pyomo: model.want_one_pool_l1 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.z[p, 20580252, 'l1'] for p in model.p) <= 1)
